text: "A just reward for defeating such a valiant fighter," you tell yourself as you pocket the silvers.

text:
Suddenly, you hear voices, seeming to come from everywhere at once.  They all sound like Baba Yaga, and they all say:
"Wheeeeeere's myyyyyy
Maaaandraaaaake Rooooooooooooot????"
speaker: baba-yaga

text: Because you failed to meet Baba Yaga's DEADline, her curse turns you into a frog on the spot, and you are forced to live out your years dodging Sauruses (Saurii?) with large feet.
speaker: narrator

text: The tingling sensation is wearing off.

text: Suddenly, the deadly poison Fragmentation Bug leaps out of a crack in the system, and injects you with its poison.  Alas, there is no cure, save to . . .

text: "Pity.  I'm in the mood for some good combat practice."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "I am a skilled teacher as well as a skilled fighter."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "However, the combat style I teach relies on the subtle alternation of Strike and Parry.  As I observe that you have no skill in the use of Parry, you would receive no benefit from my lessons."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "For a mere gold coin, I will be glad to give you some practice and some pointers on how to better your skills."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Will you pay for a lesson?"
speaker: weapon-master

text: You pay the Weapon Master's price and then...
speaker: narrator

text: That's easy to say, but you don't HAVE a gold coin.  You make your apologies to the Weapon Master.
speaker: narrator

text: "Do not ramble, my friend"
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Will you pay?"
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Will you fight?"
speaker: weapon-master

text: "I'm sorry.  Since you have no skill in parrying blows, you would receive no benefit from my instruction."
speaker: weapon-master

text: The man has the muscles of a trained athlete and is apparently quite skilled with the sword.
speaker: narrator

text: The weapon master carries a blunted practice sword, in the belief that students retain more of their learning if they survive the practice sessions.
speaker: narrator

text: "Please don't bore me, young adventurer.  Ask me about something interesting to me."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Fighting is both an art and a skill."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "It is a skill in the use of the muscle strength and agility's control."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "It is an art in the out-witting and out-maneuvering of the opponent."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "I am, of course, a most skilled artist."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Do you fight?"
speaker: weapon-master

text: "If you were a swordsman skilled in both attack and parry, we could have had a practice bout."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "The sword is the finest of all weapons.  It requires an equal measure of skill and strength."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "I am the Weapon Master."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "You will find them very skilled at fighting.  After all, I am the one who trains all of the guards at this castle."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "The Baron had the wisdom to recognize greatness when I presented myself.  Thus he made me Weapon Master."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "The Baronet had some minor talent with weapons, but he was too impatient.  If he had but listened and learned what I teach, he would never have been lost."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "I never use a shield or armor under most circumstances.  It only gets in the way of my agility and dodging ability.  It is best used by those who are strong, but not swift or agile."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "Practice is the way to refine and improve one's skills."
speaker: weapon-master

text: "If you are addressing me, sir, you must respect me enough to address me to my face!"
speaker: weapon-master
